title: Pana 100pcs Cosmetic Make Up Disposable Plastic 2.5" Spatulas Skin Care Facial Cream Mask Spatula (100 Pieces in a Container) (APPLE GREEN)
description:
About this item
Designed to be both cute and suitable for gripping, making it more comfortable and convenient to use
[Quality] Environmentally low-impact plastic that is designed for clean and easy application
[Quantity] 100 Pieces
[Measurements] 2.5" Length x 0.3" Width
[Easy to Use] Mix or apply make-up or skin care products with this disposable plastic angled spatula. Great for DIY cosmetic products, makeup application, sampling beauty products and more.
reviews_5s:
Disclaimer:
This is an honest review!
I bought the spatulas to use for scooping my raw unrefined body butters.
I love using raw unrefined shea butter and even mango butter as a moisturizer, and wanted a cleaner way to scoop them out of there bags/jars.
My thoughts:
While these are definitely small and may not be useful for some people, I found these to be quite usable. They’re small enough to be placed on the inside lid of a jar if there is any, so you don’t lose the one you are using for that specific jar.
While they are not shaped like spoons and so maybe hard to scoop with, I have managed to do so pretty successfully. It just takes a little bit of practice and gettingUsed to learning how to scoop with them.
Pros:
• Easy to use
• Comes in a nice jar, which makes it easy to open
• You get 100 little spatulas in each jar
• Can be used for many purposes
Cons:
• For some, the fact that they are not made like spoons might make them not the best for scooping lotions, etc. They may be more suitable for using with masks, etcNew Year, bunch of brand new and expensive creams, cleansers, other makeup items and devices. But this time I was determined not to stick my fingers into the jars. Originally, I was using little ice cream tester spoons, but then I decided to get something intended for this purpose and disposable. I know some people won't appreciate single use for a variety of reasons, but I wanted to protect my creams from bacteria and waste by putting my fingers in the jars, and these are perfect. I love that they come in their own jar for storage, so they're always clean and handy. They not only help to divvy out the products in the right increments, but also prevent bacteria and other nastiness that comes from using your hands, even when your hands have been washed. I've had creams go rancid, smell bad, and become unuseable in the past, and this year (and in the future) I'm determined to protect my jars of creams and serums. I find myself constantly finding uses for these, today in a tub of sugar scrub that normally I would just keep by the shower and shove my hands into and use. They are strong enough to dip out more of a body cleaning size of the scrub.

Having them handy and visible is a nice reminder to keep my new year skin care commitment morning and night.

I can definitely recommend these and plan on buying them again when the first jar is finished, so I never run out.Great product gets into small spaces saves waste.These helped me out in an amazing way! Great for scooping out small amounts of salves or topicals to put into small samples jars. And, can package as part of the bundle so the customer has a way of scooping out of the sample jar.I purchased these to apply my lip scrub at night because I don’t like putting my finger into the little jar. Not only does your finger get sticky, but then your contaminating the lip scrub with whatever may be on your finger each time! These are the perfect size and there are so many they will last for months. Highly recommend!They are very useful for when i do not want to use my fingers for my makeup or moisturizerI have ordered these a few times now for my solo esthetician practice! I use them for scooping out masks and enzymes, as well as for lotions and other products from testers. They are a great way to keep things hygienic, and I love the blue color.Good for keeping cream and ointment sterile rather than using finger.These are great…. They could be better just an inch longer or if they came with a dispenser (for sanitary reasons), but I’m definitely overall satisfied. Used them to apply lubricants and ointments for tattoo purposes. Yeah, I know ”we” usually use popsicle sticks but I was boujie, loving the pink and they weren’t expensive. My female customers loved the detail. ;)Is exactly what is described and doesn’t have a foul plastic smell
reviews_1s: First shipment was one container and that one was most of the spatulas in the bottom of the bag. The order was for TWO containers. Received one replacement. I paid for two. I’d seriously think before ordering. I’ve ordered these several times and loved them. This time I’m very disappointed! Where is the second container???I ordered these because they showed them in a container with a screw top lid. I did not want them in packages. I could have spent $2.00 and got twice the amount. Or I could have bought them from a different seller. These are worthless to me. Four little packages to lose rather than a container to put on the counter. Showing an inaccurate picture is the same as lying.Manufacturer needs to find some way to seal these for safety reasons. These could be used for lip products and need to be kept hygienic before use by the customer.The pieces are exactly what I wanted, they didn't come sealed like one other commenter mentioned but I think the worst part is they have the strongest smell of petroleum or whatever that smell is (like they were picked up from an automotive store). I feel like using them wouldn't be safe. Soaked them in soapy water for a week or more and they still never lost that smell. I didn't feel like they would be safe to use so I tossed them. What a waste of money.This product did not come with a “safety” seal and the spatulas have lint/dirt particles on them.I ordered 200, received 100. Had I only wanted 100, I wouldn't have ordered a larger package. Very dissatisfiedDid not come sealed. Cobrainer looks like it was opened.Awful, super upset when I opened the package and saw that the container was opened. I’ve seen pictures of other people but I thought they would have upped a notch on the packaging and sealing better but I guess not. I will be returning for sure.I’m giving this product one star because while it serves its intended purpose, I’m throwing the entire package out. I opened it and a strong blast of what smells like diesel fuel smacked me in the face. Even after washing them, they still smell awful. Wouldn’t want to hurt my skin or ruin my products over that!These are tiny and absolutely useless. The only thing I can think of to use them for is to mix resin. They’re too short to scrape remaining foundation from the jar and they bend to easily anyways. No good for anything that I can think of.
